stem: A factory produces food packages using two sources. Packaging Line Morning Shift handles 68% of production with a known defect rate of 4.8%. Packaging Line Night Shift handles 32% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 4.9% and 8.3%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected food package is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Packaging Line Night Shift?

ground_truth.answer: P(Packaging Line Night Shift | defective) ranges from 0.325 to 0.449 as Packaging Line Night Shift's defect rate varies from 4.9% to 8.3%. Point estimate at 6.6%: 0.393.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.325, 0.449] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
